calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Appointment Of Neutral Appraiser And To Disqualify Respondents Party Appraiser In Insurance Appraisal Pursuant To Insurance Code Section 2071 & Payment Of Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Jul-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER 455 SOUTH SECOND STREET, LLC, A CORPORATION's Petition To Compel Appointment Of Neutral Appraiser And To Disqualify Respondents Party Appraiser In Insurance Appraisal Pursuant To Insurance Code Section 2071 & Payment Of Court Reporter Fee.

Petitioner 455 South Second Street, LLC's petition to compel appointment of neutral appraiser and to disqualify respondent's party appraiser in insurance appraisal pursuant to Insurance Code Section 2071 is granted in part, as follows.

The court declines to disqualify John Brinkley as Respondent Century-National Insurance Company's party appraiser. There is not a substantial business relationship between Brinkley and CNIC. (See Mahnke v. Superior Court (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 565, 579-582; see Brinkley Decl. pars. 5-7.) Likewise, the court declines to order Century to appoint a new appraiser, or to provide all communications with Brinkley regarding the appraisal to petitioner.

Insurance Code section 2071 provides that if the party-selected appraisers fail for 15 days to agree upon the umpire, "then, on request of the insured or this company, the umpire shall be selected by a judge of a court of record in the state in which the property covered is located." Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6 provides that when a petition is made to a court to appoint a neutral arbitrator, "the court shall nominate five persons from lists of persons supplied jointly by the parties to the arbitration or obtained from a governmental agency concerned with arbitration or private disinterested association concerned with arbitration." (Code Civ. Proc. 1281.6.)

The Court hereby nominates the following five persons from the lists supplied by the parties: Bruce A. Edwards, Esq. (JAMS); Michael J. Timpane, Esq. (AAA); Zela Claiborne, Esq. (JAMS); Hon. Lynn Duryee (Ret.) (JAMS); and Hon. James Lambden (Ret.) (ADR). "The parties to the agreement . . . may within five days of receipt of notice of the nominees from the court jointly selected the arbitrator whether or not the arbitrator is among the nominees." (Id.) "If the parties fail to select an arbitrator within the five-day period, the court shall appoint the arbitrator from the nominees." (Id.)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
